data.title: China's review of Meta's acquisition of Manus: Core, Boundaries, and Future Direction
data.description:
Author: Zhang Feng

Recently, social media giant Meta (formerly Facebook)'s acquisition plan of the Chinese multimodal AI project Manus (note: not the British/Irish mixed reality project) has entered the review process of China's Ministry of Commerce. This case not only involves the balancing of commercial interests, but also touches on sensitive issues of national security, technological sovereignty, and data boundaries.

I. Core Rules and Basis: The Legal Framework of China's Review

China's review of foreign mergers and acquisitions is based on a multi-layered legal and regulatory system, mainly based on laws such as the Anti-Monopoly Law, the Foreign Investment Law, the Cybersecurity Law, the Data Security Law, the Personal Information Protection Law, and the Catalogue of Technologies Prohibited and Restricted from Export.

China's review of foreign mergers and acquisitions is based on a multi-layered legal and regulatory system, mainly based on laws such as the Anti-Monopoly Law, the Foreign Investment Law, the Cybersecurity Law, the Data Security Law, the Personal Information Protection Law, and the Catalogue of Technologies Prohibited and Restricted from Export.

These laws and regulations collectively form the core rule basis for reviewing Meta's acquisition of Manus. First, the Anti-Monopoly Law requires review of mergers that may have the effect of excluding or restricting competition. As a global giant in social media and the metaverse field, Meta's acquisition could raise concerns about monopolies in China and even globally. Although Manus is a small startup, if its technology has disruptive potential, the acquisition could strengthen Meta's market dominance in the AI ​​field. China's Ministry of Commerce will assess the impact of the transaction on competition in relevant markets, especially in the emerging field of multimodal AI. Second, the Foreign Investment Law and its supporting Measures for Security Review of Foreign Investment establish a national security review system. This system focuses on the impact of foreign mergers and acquisitions on national defense security, key technologies, important infrastructure, and important products and services. Multimodal AI technology integrates visual, voice, and text processing capabilities, possessing broad application potential in military, public security, and social governance, and is highly likely to trigger national security reviews. Third, the Cybersecurity Law, the Data Security Law, and the Personal Information Protection Law constitute the "three pillars" of China's data governance. Multimodal AI projects typically require massive amounts of data for training, which may include sensitive data such as Chinese users' personal information, geographic information, and biometrics. As a Chinese-based project, Manus likely accumulated such data during its operation. If Meta acquires and transfers this data overseas, it could jeopardize China's national security and public interests. Therefore, local data storage and outbound security assessments will be key aspects of the review process. Finally, the Catalogue of Technologies Prohibited and Restricted from Export clearly defines China's export control requirements for sensitive technologies. Multimodal AI technologies may involve cutting-edge fields such as computer vision, speech recognition, and natural language processing. If these technologies are included in the catalog, their cross-border transfer will be strictly restricted. Even if the transaction itself is a merger and acquisition rather than a direct export, the transfer of technology control may be considered a de facto technology export, thus triggering compliance review. In summary, the Meta acquisition of Manus will face multiple reviews related to antitrust, national security, data security, and technology export controls. These rules are intertwined and jointly determine the depth and breadth of the review. II. The Core and Boundaries of the Review: Balancing Security and Development In the specific review, the Chinese Ministry of Commerce will define clear review boundaries around the two core principles of "security" and "development." On the one hand, China is committed to safeguarding national security and technological sovereignty; on the other hand, China also adheres to opening up to the outside world and encourages scientific and technological innovation and cooperation. The key to the review lies in finding a balance between these two. Core 1: National Security and Data Sovereignty. The dual-use nature (civilian and military) of multimodal AI technology makes it a focus of national security review. Does Manus's technology involve sensitive applications such as facial recognition, behavioral analysis, and affective computing? Could its algorithms be used for surveillance, public opinion manipulation, or military purposes? These are all questions that must be clarified during the review. Furthermore, the issue of data sovereignty is particularly prominent. Chinese law requires operators of critical information infrastructure to store personal information and important data within China, and to undergo a security assessment before transferring them abroad. As a US company, Meta is bound by domestic laws such as the CLOUD Act and may be required by the US government to provide data. This data jurisdiction conflict has made Chinese regulators highly vigilant about the risks of data leaving the country. The review may require Meta to commit to establishing an independent data storage and processing center in China and accepting supervision from the Chinese government. Core Two: Technological Autonomy and Industrial Competition. China is vigorously promoting technological self-reliance, especially in strategic areas such as AI. As a domestic innovation project, is Manus's technology considered "critical technology"? Will the acquisition lead to the loss of Chinese technology and weaken domestic innovation capabilities? The review will assess the impact of the transaction on China's AI industry chain. If Manus's technology is unique and internationally leading, the acquisition may be seen as a threat to China's long-term technological competitiveness. Conversely, if Manus's technology is still in its early stages and the acquisition brings funding, talent, and international market opportunities, the review process may be more inclined to approve it. The boundary lies in distinguishing between "benign technological cooperation" and "malicious technology plunder." China's Ministry of Commerce may attach conditions, such as requiring Meta to retain Manus's R&D team operating in China or to cooperate with Chinese research institutions to ensure the technology spillover effect. Core Three: Market Fairness and Consumer Welfare. Antitrust reviews focus on whether the acquisition will substantially reduce competition. Meta already holds a dominant position in areas such as social media and virtual reality. If it further integrates multimodal AI capabilities through the acquisition of Manus, it may strengthen its ecosystem barriers and suppress domestic Chinese competitors. The review will analyze the definition of the relevant market, changes in market share, and potential anti-competitive effects (such as a destructive acquisition). The boundary lies in determining whether the transaction truly harms consumer interests. If the acquisition promotes technological innovation, lowers prices, or improves service quality, it may be approved. However, if Meta intends to shut down Manus or shelve the technology to eliminate potential threats after the acquisition, it may be rejected. Core Four: Geopolitical and Compliance Risks. Currently, Sino-US technological competition is intensifying, and the US technological blockade against China continues to escalate. When reviewing this case, China will inevitably consider geopolitical factors. Although Meta is a private company, its connection with the US government cannot be ignored. The review will assess whether the acquisition implies a US strategy of technological containment against China. At the same time, Meta must demonstrate its ability to comply with Chinese laws, particularly data security and content moderation requirements. Historically, Meta's platforms have faced access restrictions in China, and its content management policies differ from Chinese regulations. These compliance risks may affect the review results. The boundary lies in distinguishing between purely commercial activities and political tools, requiring Meta to make legally binding compliance commitments. In summary, the core of the review is to prudently assess the impact of the transaction on industry competition and consumer welfare, while mitigating geopolitical risks, under the premise of safeguarding national security, data sovereignty, and technological autonomy. The review boundaries are jointly defined by legal provisions, technological sensitivity, market structure, and the international situation. III. Technology Export Control Compliance: A Sensitive Dimension of Multimodal AI Technology export control is another key dimension in reviewing the Meta acquisition of Manus. China implements a catalog system for technology exports, with the "Catalogue of Technologies Prohibited and Restricted from Export" serving as the direct basis. Whether multimodal AI technology is included in the catalogue will determine the compliance threshold for transactions. According to the 2020 revised catalogue, the restricted export section includes "speech synthesis technology" and "artificial intelligence interactive interface technology" under the "information processing technology" category, while the prohibited export section involves "cryptographic security technology," etc. As an interdisciplinary field, multimodal AI may involve several restricted technologies: Computer vision technology, such as facial recognition and behavior recognition, may be used for public safety monitoring and is considered a sensitive technology. Speech Recognition and Synthesis Technology: Involves speech data processing and may be used for eavesdropping or public opinion analysis. Natural Language Processing Technology: Especially for semantic understanding and sentiment analysis of Chinese, which may involve information content security. Multimodal Fusion Algorithm: Performs correlation analysis of visual, speech, and text information, exhibiting higher technical sensitivity. If Manus's core technology falls into the aforementioned category, the transfer of control to Meta requires prior approval from the provincial-level commerce department, and ultimately review by the Ministry of Commerce. The review will focus on: whether the technology has potential for military use, whether it affects China's technological leadership, and whether it violates China's international obligations. Furthermore, even if the technology is not explicitly listed in the catalog, if the review deems it "sensitive," it may still invoke regulations such as the Data Security Law to intervene. In terms of compliance, Meta and Manus must jointly submit a technology export application, detailing the technology's content, application scenarios, and transfer methods. The Ministry of Commerce will organize expert reviews to assess the risks of technology export. Possible compliance conditions include: requiring the technology not to be used for military purposes, restricting subsequent transfers, and requiring a backup R&D team to be maintained in China. If the technology is deemed prohibited from export, the transaction will be directly blocked. It is worth noting that the United States also has strict export controls on technology to China, especially on AI chips and software tools. After the acquisition, Meta may integrate Manus's technology into its global R&D system, which could lead to the indirect flow of technology to restricted areas in the United States, triggering a conflict of dual controls between China and the United States. Therefore, China will also consider the potential chain of technology outflow during the review process. IV. Future Direction of the Acquisition Based on the above analysis, the prospects of Meta's acquisition of Manus are full of variables and the following scenarios may occur: Scenario 1: Conditional Approval. This is the most likely outcome. The Chinese Ministry of Commerce may approve the transaction based on the principle of "prudent control," but with strict conditions. For example: requiring Manus's data servers to remain within China, under Chinese supervision; requiring Meta to commit to continued investment in Manus's research in China, and retaining the core R&D team; prohibiting the use of Manus technology in military or sensitive areas; requiring Meta to conduct technological cooperation with Chinese AI companies, etc. This outcome satisfies Meta's commercial needs while safeguarding China's security interests. Scenario Two: Transaction Prohibited. If the review determines that Manus technology involves core areas of national security, or that the risk of data leaving the country cannot be mitigated through conditions, the transaction may be directly prohibited. Especially against the backdrop of escalating tensions in Sino-US relations, if Manus technology is deemed to have strategic value, the likelihood of a prohibition will increase. Furthermore, if Meta cannot prove its ability to effectively comply with Chinese data laws, the deal may also fail. Scenario 3: Meta voluntarily withdraws its investment. Faced with lengthy and uncertain reviews, and potentially stringent compliance conditions, Meta may weigh the pros and cons and voluntarily abandon the acquisition. Especially if the attached conditions severely restrict Meta's integration and application of Manus technology, weakening the commercial value of the acquisition, withdrawal would be a rational choice. In addition, geopolitical pressure may also prompt Meta to avoid getting involved in the US-China technology dispute. Scenario 4: Transaction restructuring. To pass the review, Meta may adjust its acquisition plan, such as changing to a joint venture or technology licensing model instead of a full acquisition. This would allow the Chinese side to retain some control over the technology and data, reducing risks. However, such restructuring may involve complex negotiations and may not align with Meta's long-term strategy. Overall, China's review of this case will reflect a combination of "bottom-line thinking" and "openness and balance." While safeguarding national security and data sovereignty, China will not easily close the door to technological cooperation, but any transaction must comply with Chinese laws and interests. In the future, with the continuous evolution of AI technology and the continuous improvement of China's regulatory system, similar cross-border M&A reviews will become more normalized and refined, becoming an important microcosm of global technological competition.

data.title: Who is paying the price for the crypto bull market?
data.description:
Author: Wan Hui, Founder of Primitive Ventures; Source: @DoveyWan; Translated by: Shaw, Jinse Finance

In 2025, the cryptocurrency industry achieved almost all of its expected goals. Structurally, it should have been a glorious year. But why does it feel...so lifeless?

It's not that "the price didn't rise" means it's all over. Bitcoin hit new highs. But the atmosphere, sentiment, internal confirmation, the follow-up of other cryptocurrencies, and retail investor enthusiasm have all changed. Perhaps most worryingly, the once "hot money-driving asset" has now lost its appeal in terms of wealth effect and volatility.

... Related cryptocurrency assets are no longer moving in sync with Bitcoin and Ethereum as they have in previous cycles: Memecoins topped the charts between Q4 2024 and Q1 2025, with the launch of the Trump token further fueling this trend. Cryptocurrency stocks peaked around the time of the Circle IPO and began to decline between May and August 2025. Most altcoins have never formed a sustained trend. There is asymmetry during the upward movement, while the downward movement is entirely driven by all participants. Zooming out further, the situation becomes even stranger. Despite a favorable policy environment, Bitcoin underperformed almost all major traditional financial assets in 2025, including gold, US stocks, Hong Kong stocks, A-shares, and even some bond benchmark indices. This marked the first time Bitcoin's performance had decoupled from all other asset classes. This divergence is crucial: prices hit new highs, but this wasn't internally confirmed, while other markets outperformed. This reveals a simple yet unsettling fact: Bitcoin's liquidity supply chain has undergone significant changes, with its original four-year settlement cycle altered by larger forces in other markets. Therefore, we will delve into who bought at the highs, who exited the market, and where the price bottom is.

The Huge Gap: Offshore vs. Onshore Funding

We experienced three distinct phases in this cycle—

Phase A (November 2024 to January 2025): Trump's election victory and a more favorable regulatory environment triggered a shared FOMO sentiment among domestic and foreign investors. Bitcoin's price broke the $100,000 mark for the first time.

Phase B (April to mid-August 2025): After a deleveraging sell-off, Bitcoin resumed its upward momentum and broke through $120,000. Phase C (Early October 2025): Bitcoin reached its current all-time high in early October, followed by a flash crash on October 11th and a period of adjustment. In each phase, we saw significant differences between offshore and onshore funding behavior. Spot: Onshore buying on breakouts, offshore selling on rallies. Coinbase Premium remained positive in Phases A, B, and C. High levels of buying demand primarily came from onshore spot funds.

  • Coinbase Bitcoin balance trended downward throughout the cycle. Available inventory on the US side decreased.

  • With the price rebound in Phase B and Phase C, Binance balance increased significantly. Offshore spot holders replenished their inventory, and potential selling pressure also increased accordingly.

  • As prices rebounded in Phase B and Phase C, Binance balance increased significantly. Offshore spot holders replenished their inventory, and potential selling pressure also increased.

Futures: Offshore Leverage Rises, Onshore Positions Decrease

  • Offshore open interest (Binance and other offshore trading platforms) experienced a surge in Phase B and Phase C. Leverage ratios increased. Even after October 10th, leverage ratios quickly fell back and recovered to or exceeded previous peaks (Figure 4). Since the beginning of 2025, onshore open interest (CME) has been declining. Institutional investors have not increased their risk exposure despite new highs in contracts. Meanwhile, Bitcoin volatility has diverged from price action. In August 2025, when Bitcoin first broke through $120,000, DVOL was near a local low. The options market did not adequately compensate for the ongoing risk.

    Each "top" seems to reflect the divergence between onshore and offshore traders. When onshore spot funds drive prices to break through, offshore spot traders take the opportunity to sell. When offshore leveraged capital chases the rally, onshore futures and options traders reduce their positions and remain on the sidelines.

    Where are the marginal buyers?

    Glassnode estimates that the amount of Bitcoin held by corporations and DAT-type instruments increased from approximately 197,000 at the beginning of 2023 to approximately 1.08 million by the end of 2025, a net increase of approximately 890,000 over two years. DAT has become one of the largest structured investment vehicles in the Bitcoin system. Another often misunderstood area is ETFs. By the end of 2025, US spot Bitcoin ETFs held approximately 1.36 million Bitcoin, an increase of approximately 23% year-over-year, representing approximately 6.8% of the circulating supply. Institutional investors (13F filers) held less than a quarter of the total number of ETFs, and most of them were hedge funds and investment advisors, clearly not the well-known "diamond hand" family.

    The Disappearing Retail Investors

    Since the beginning of 2025, retail investor traffic on Binance, Coinbase, and other top exchanges has been steadily declining, clearly indicating a market trend. Retail investor fatigue has persisted since Trump's crackdown on memecoins, and overall retail investor sentiment has been technically bearish since the beginning of 2024.

    Speculators' funds did not rotate into alternative investments. Many exited entirely, returning to the stock volatility market, while new speculators happily profited in the US stock market or their home stock market.

    Even South Korean retail investors sold off Upbit and instead bet on the KOSPI index and US stocks: Upbit's average daily trading volume in 2025 decreased by about 80% compared to 2024. During the same period, the KOSPI index rose by more than 75%. South Korean retail investors net bought approximately $31 billion worth of US stocks.

    Sellers

    Each cycle has established whales selling at local highs, but interestingly, the timing of this cycle's selling coincided with the RS divergence.

    Bitcoin had long been closely correlated with US growth tech stocks until around August 2025, when it began to significantly lag behind ARKK and Nvidia, followed by the crash on October 10th, a gap it has yet to close. Just before this divergence, in late July, Galaxy disclosed in its financial reports and media briefings that it had executed a sell order for over 80,000 Bitcoins on behalf of a long-time holder. This transaction brought the phenomenon of "Satoshi-era whales taking profits" into the public eye.

    Miners Sell Assets to Fund AI Capital Expenditures

    From the Bitcoin halving in 2024 to the end of 2025, miner reserves experienced the most sustained decline since 2021. By the end of the year, reserves stood at approximately 1.806 million Bitcoins. Hashrate decreased by approximately 15% year-over-year.

  • Horizontal Analysis: Valuation and Premium Levels of DAT, ETFs, and Other Bitcoin Alternatives

Early winners of Bitcoin, including veteran players, miners, and Asian gray whales, are distributing tokens to passive ETF holders, DAT structures, and long-term national capital. Bitcoin's trajectory appears to resemble that of FAANG between 2013 and 2020.

The market is slowly shifting from high-beta investment strategies dominated by early retail and growth funds to passive allocation strategies led by index funds, pension funds, and sovereign wealth funds. Bitcoin is now an easily accessible crypto asset without any physical contact with cryptocurrency. You can buy it through a brokerage account, manage it like an ETF, with clear accounting and the ability to explain it to a trader's investment committee in five sentences. The valuations of most other crypto assets are not based on their actual utility or legitimacy in the physical market and on Wall Street. We always expect a new bull market, but it would be ideal if this bull market were not just about price increases, but about utility increases, translating the legitimacy of the ETF era into on-chain demand, passive holding into active use, and delivering real returns, rather than just a constantly shifting narrative. If this happens, today's "top buyers" will look less like late-cycle buyers and more like early investors in a new cycle. Bitcoin will eventually become a national reserve. Code is devouring banks. Cryptocurrency still needs to evolve into a new tool of civilization.

data.title: What impact will CARF have on the crypto ecosystem?
data.description:
In the past two days, many articles online have suddenly started discussing a relatively unfamiliar term.CARF.

CARFThe full name of CARF is Crypto Asset Reporting Framework, which is a global automatic exchange protocol for tax information specifically for cryptocurrency transactions..

From the literal meaning alone, we can guess what it does. It's a tool specifically designed to tax users of crypto assets. Why has this term suddenly become a hot topic online these past few days? Because starting next year (i.e., 2027), countries that have already signed this agreement will begin implementing automatic information exchange between nations based on it. Tax residents of any country that has joined this system are likely to be taxed based on this information exchange system if they trade crypto assets. To understand this protocol, we can start with the following key points: The protocol's focus is currently on centralized exchanges (CEXs). That is, all users who trade on CEXs are potential taxpayers. All active and passive earnings obtained on CEX may be considered potential taxable assets. Active earnings refer to earnings from various user-initiated transactions, such as earnings from transactions between fiat and crypto assets, and between crypto assets. The so-called passive income refers to income obtained through airdrops, wealth management, etc. In other words, any change in a user's balance in CEX, whether active or passive income, may be taxed. Furthermore, withdrawals initiated from CEX may also be recorded and tracked. The above analysis focuses on the behaviors targeted by the protocol. Next, let's look at the regions covered by the agreement. This agreement is only valid for countries and regions that have joined it. Currently, approximately 40 countries and regions have joined the agreement, and these countries and regions will conduct their first information exchange next year (2027). The Hong Kong government is reportedly set to join the agreement soon and plans to automatically exchange information under the agreement starting in 2028. There has been no indication that mainland China will join the agreement. Will mainland China tax residents (those who only pay taxes in mainland China) be affected by this agreement? There are currently two viewpoints on this point: One viewpoint holds that because mainland China has not yet joined the agreement, and there is currently no publicly available and consistent definition of crypto assets, tax residents of mainland China are not currently affected by this agreement. However, another viewpoint holds that although crypto assets are in a gray area in mainland China, the law can also interpret the "taxation" arising from crypto asset transactions in other ways, so this impact cannot be completely ignored. Is there any way to minimize the impact of this protocol? I think there are currently some ways. Because this protocol currently only applies to CEX, if a user doesn't conduct transactions or activities on CEX, then theoretically they can be relatively unaffected by this protocol. To avoid leaving a trace on CEXs, the best way is to withdraw your assets and fully adapt to a purely on-chain lifestyle in the future. Actually, this is also my consistent view: Since you've already entered the crypto ecosystem, why cling to CEXs and refuse to let go? The current on-chain world already possesses powerful functionalities. Whether it's cross-chain communication or various activities, the functions provided by the blockchain can fully meet most daily needs. We should strive to eliminate CEX from our crypto lives as much as possible.

data.title: US Senate Bill Limits Stablecoin Yields; Franklin Templeton Upgrade Fund
data.description:
Headline

▌US Senate Crypto Market Structure Bill Proposes Restrictions on Stablecoin Yields, May Face Stricter Amendments

US Senate Banking Committee Chairman Tim Scott released the 278-page text of the Crypto Market Structure Bill, proposing to divide regulatory power over digital assets between the SEC and CFTC, and clarify the securities and commodities attributes. The issue of stablecoin yields has become a core point of contention. The draft prohibits digital asset service providers from paying any interest or yields to users who only hold payment-type stablecoins, but allows for exceptions based on incentives such as trading, staking, liquidity provision, or collateral. Sources say that stricter amendments, which may significantly restrict stablecoin yields, may be submitted and could be passed. Meanwhile, ethical clauses concerning Trump and his family's crypto businesses remain unresolved, which Democrats consider a legislative "red line." The bill will enter the revision and voting stage in the committee this week.

Franklin Templeton Upgrades Two Money Market Funds to Facilitate Blockchain and Stablecoin Applications Franklin Templeton announced updates to two of its institutional-grade money market funds to make them available for the emerging tokenized finance and regulated stablecoin markets. The changes involve funds managed by its affiliate, Western Asset Management, and aim to help institutional clients use familiar cash management tools within blockchain platforms and stablecoin reserve frameworks. The Western Asset Institutional Treasury Obligations Fund has adjusted its investment structure to comply with the stablecoin reserve asset requirements of the U.S. GENIUS Act, currently holding only U.S. Treasury securities with maturities of no more than 93 days, which can be used as reserve assets for compliant stablecoins. The other fund, the Western Asset Institutional Treasury Reserves Fund, has launched a new digital institutional share class, allowing approved intermediaries to record and transfer fund share ownership through blockchain infrastructure. Franklin Templeton stated that this move aims to help institutional investors gradually adopt on-chain infrastructure without introducing entirely new crypto-native products. This development also echoes recent moves by large financial institutions such as JPMorgan Chase to launch tokenized money market products on Ethereum. As of press time, according to CoinGecko data: BTC price is $95,339.80, a 24-hour change of +4.6%; ETH price is $3,325.90, a 24-hour change of +7.7%; BNB price is $943.32, a 24-hour change of +4.2%. SOL price is $146.12, up 5.0% in the last 24 hours; DOGE price is $0.1486, up 8.7% in the last 24 hours; XRP price is $2.16, up 5.4% in the last 24 hours; TRX price is $0.3064, up 2.3% in the last 24 hours. WLFI price is $0.1805, up 6.8% in the last 24 hours; HYPE price is $25.57, up 7.8% in the last 24 hours. Policy The committee announced on Tuesday that the hearing, originally scheduled for January 15 and postponed to Monday, would be held at 3 p.m. that day. This hearing is a crucial step in advancing the legislation, allowing lawmakers to debate amendments, vote on whether to include them in the full bill, and then vote on whether to submit the complete bill to the full Senate for consideration. The Senate Banking Committee will hold a hearing on its version of the bill this Thursday. The draft bill from the Banking Committee was released around midnight Monday, but lawmakers are expected to submit amendments before the hearing. Senator Elizabeth Warren: Trump-owned WLFI bank application should be suspended. Senator Elizabeth Warren has called for a suspension of bank applications related to World Liberty Financial (WLFI) until President Trump divests his family's stake in the company and related digital asset businesses. Warren, a senior Democrat on the Senate Banking Committee, made this request before the committee's planned consideration of the cryptocurrency market structure bill, citing significant ethical concerns regarding the application. In a letter to Jonathan Gould, the Director General of the Office of the Comptroller of the Currency, Warren stated that approving the bank's application could allow the president to directly influence his own company's profits and regulate its operations, raising ethical concerns for the government. The UK Financial Conduct Authority (FCA) has expressed concern about the sale of complex ETPs to retail investors, warning of gaps in risk disclosure and customer due diligence that could undermine the protections afforded under the Consumer Duty. In a review report released on January 12, the FCA examined how companies of different sizes and business models market and distribute complex ETPs to individual investors. The report revealed inconsistencies: some companies practiced well, clearly targeting their markets, rigorously assessing customer knowledge, and continuously monitoring purchases; however, others relied on superficial or weak checks and disclosures, failing to effectively help retail investors understand the associated risks. The FCA stated that these weaknesses could expose investors to product risks they do not fully understand. Complex ETPs represent a small percentage of the overall market but are high-risk. These include leveraged and inverse strategies, as well as exchange-traded notes (ETNs) linked to cryptocurrencies. These product structures can amplify returns, but can also accelerate losses if held for too long or in volatile markets.

Blockchain Applications

▌Sources: CoinGecko Considering Sale at Approximately $500 Million Valuation

According to sources, cryptocurrency data platform CoinGecko is considering a sale and has hired investment bank Moelis to handle the process. The cryptocurrency market data company is valued at approximately $500 million. This potential sale comes at a time of rapidly accelerating cryptocurrency M&A activity, with disclosed M&A deals reaching approximately $8.6 billion in 2025, totaling a record 133 transactions.

▌SSPAC Affiliated with Kraken Files for $250 Million IPO

KrakAcquisition, a new blank-check company backed by Kraken, Tribe Capital, and Natural Capital, filed documents with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) on Monday, planning to raise up to $250 million through an initial public offering (IPO). According to the SEC filing, KrakAcquisition, incorporated in the Cayman Islands in July 2025, is an exempt company and plans to issue 25 million units at $10 each, and is expected to apply for listing on the Nasdaq Global Market under the ticker symbol “KRAQU”. While the SPAC targets companies in the crypto infrastructure sector, Kraken reportedly filed a separate confidential Form S-1 in November seeking to list its common stock in a potential IPO.

... Coinbase International Exchange to Undergo Maintenance on January 24th, Trading Suspended Coinbase International Exchange will undergo system maintenance at 17:00 UTC on January 24th, which is expected to be completed within 3 hours. During the maintenance, all trading and transfer functions will be suspended. Users should complete their operations in advance and check their order status. Trading will gradually resume through price discovery auctions. Cryptocurrency

JPMorgan Chase CFO Warns of Stablecoin Interest-Paying Risks: A “Parallel Banking System Without Regulation”

JPMorgan Chase Chief Financial Officer Jeremy Barnum stated during the Q4 earnings call that the practice of offering stablecoin yields to users in the crypto industry is “clearly dangerous and undesirable,” arguing that this is equivalent to building a “parallel banking system” with features of banks, such as interest-paying deposits, in the absence of prudent regulation. He pointed out that such products could bring systemic risks similar to bank deposits, but are not bound by centuries of banking regulatory frameworks. Barnum’s remarks come as the U.S. Senate Banking Committee released a new draft bill on the structure of the crypto market, which proposes to restrict stablecoin issuers and crypto platforms from directly offering yields to users unless linked to specific activities such as staking. Barnum emphasized that JPMorgan Chase is willing to compete with crypto products, but only if the relevant services operate within a suitable regulatory framework.

▌Grayscale Updates Q1 2026 "Assets Under Consideration" List, Adding 36 Altcoins

Grayscale has updated a list of assets under consideration for future investment products, as well as a new list of existing assets. The "Assets Under Consideration" list includes digital assets not currently included in Grayscale's investment products, but which the team has identified as potential candidates for future inclusion. This list adds 36 altcoins, with candidate companies spanning five major sectors: smart contracts, finance, consumer and culture, artificial intelligence, and utilities and services. Smart contract platforms and finance have the most assets listed. However, inclusion on this list does not guarantee that an asset will be included in an investment portfolio; it only indicates that Grayscale is actively evaluating these assets.

... BlackRock Deposits 3290 BTC and 5692 ETH into Coinbase According to Arkham data, BlackRock deposited 3290 BTC ($302.9 million) and 5692 ETH ($17.82 million) into Coinbase, with further transfers expected. Vitalik Again Exchanges Some Free Tokens for 9.4 ETH According to Lookonchain, Ethereum founder Vitalik sold some of his free tokens from his wallet in the past 30 minutes, receiving 9.4 ETH (approximately $29,400). Strive will hold nearly 12,800 bitcoins after acquiring Semler. Strive shareholders have approved the acquisition of Semler Scientific, but investors were surprised by the 1-for-20 reverse stock split accompanying the deal, causing Strive's stock price to fall by 12% and Semler's stock price to fall by nearly 10%. Following the merger, Strive will hold approximately 12,798 bitcoins, exceeding the holdings of Tesla and Trump Media & Technology Group. Strive plans to profit from Semler's medical diagnostics business and repay approximately $120 million in debt, while maintaining a streamlined corporate structure and focusing on its Bitcoin business and revenue generation.

▌XRP, SOL, and 6 other tokens will enjoy the same treatment as BTC and ETH from the date the Clarity Act takes effect

According to crypto journalist Eleanor Terrett, the draft Clarity Act contains a noteworthy clause based on whether certain tokens have been included in ETFs by January 1, 2026, thus classifying them as non-ancillary assets. The clause stipulates that if a token is already a core asset of an ETF by January 1, 2026, and that ETF is listed on a national stock exchange and registered under Section 6 of the Securities Exchange Act, then that token will not be subject to the information disclosure obligations required of other tokens. In other words, under this framework, XRP, SOL, LTC, HBAR, DOGE, and LINK will be treated the same as BTC and ETH from the date the act takes effect.

▌Coinbase to Launch Raydium (RAY) Spot Trading

Coinbase will launch Raydium (RAY) spot trading. The RAY-USD trading pair will begin trading if liquidity conditions are met and the supported trading regions meet the requirements.

Important Economic Developments

▌US December CPI Rises 2.7% Year-on-Year, Meeting Market Expectations

The US Consumer Price Index (CPI) rose 2.7% year-on-year in December, in line with market expectations and unchanged from the previous month.

▌Following CPI Release, the Probability of a Fed Rate Cut in April Rises to 42%

On Tuesday, traders increased their bets, believing the Fed might not wait until Chairman Powell's term ends in May before cutting rates, after the Bureau of Labor Statistics reported that basic consumer prices rose slightly less than expected. Although traders still believe a June rate cut is the most likely outcome, the latest data predicts a 42% probability of a Fed rate cut in April, up from 38% before the data release.

▌Fed Mouthpiece: December CPI Unlikely to Change Fed's Current Wait-and-See Stance

Fed mouthpiece Nick Timiraos said the December Consumer Price Index (CPI) is unlikely to change the Fed's current wait-and-see stance, as officials are likely to want to see more evidence that inflation is stabilizing and gradually declining before cutting rates.

The Federal Reserve has cut its benchmark interest rate in three consecutive meetings, most recently in December, even though inflation stopped falling last year. Officials lowered rates due to concerns about a potentially larger-than-expected slowdown in the labor market. For rate cuts to resume, Fed officials may need to see new evidence that labor market conditions are deteriorating or that price pressures are easing. The latter may require at least several more months of inflation data to become apparent.

▌Trump: Fed Chair Nominee to be Determined in Coming Weeks

President Trump stated that the Fed Chair nominee will be determined in the coming weeks.

▌Fed Chair Candidate Rieder Speaks Out: Supports Rate Cut to 3%

BlackRock's Global Chief Fixed Income Investment Officer, Rick Rieder, a potential successor to Fed Chair Powell, is reportedly scheduled to meet with President Trump on Thursday.

At this juncture, Riddell reiterated his support for lowering the U.S. benchmark interest rate to 3%, a level that would be the lowest in more than three years. In a CNBC interview aired on Monday, Riddell stated that he had repeatedly expressed his desire for rates to fall to 3% for months. He reiterated his support for this move on Monday, which would reduce borrowing costs by at least 50 basis points (0.5 percentage points) from current levels. The current target range for the federal funds rate is 3.5%–3.75%, following a 25-basis-point rate cut by Federal Reserve officials in December. “I think the Fed does have some policy room,” Riddell said. “My position has been very clear over the past few months. The Fed must lower rates, and I don’t think it needs to be lowered too much, eventually to 3%—that level is closer to the neutral rate.” The neutral rate is a theoretical level of borrowing costs that is neither stimulative nor restrictive, and can maintain the smooth operation of the U.S. economy.

▌The probability of the Fed keeping interest rates unchanged in January is 97.2%

According to CME's "FedWatch": The probability of the Fed cutting interest rates by 25 basis points in January is 2.8%, and the probability of keeping interest rates unchanged is 97.2%. By March, the cumulative probability of a 25 basis point rate cut is 26.8%, the probability of keeping interest rates unchanged is 72.5%, and the cumulative probability of a 50 basis point rate cut is 0.7%.

Golden Encyclopedia

▌Why is DAT Treasury stock falling faster than its asset holdings?

DAT Treasury's reserve strategy has been effective during market rallies. Stocks of companies holding large amounts of cryptocurrency assets tend to outperform their asset holdings, attracting investors who want to invest in cryptocurrencies but do not want to hold them directly.

However, this relationship collapses dramatically during market downturns. Crypto treasury stocks tend to fall more sharply than the cryptocurrencies they hold. This pattern is not accidental but stems from the interplay between the stock market, corporate balance sheets, and investor behavior. Theoretically, if a company holds a significant amount of cryptocurrency, its market capitalization should move in tandem with the asset's price. But in reality, history has shown otherwise. During market sell-offs, these stocks often underperform the cryptocurrencies they hold. This is because buying these stocks is not the same as buying Bitcoin. Investors are buying equity in a highly leveraged, market-sentiment-driven company that holds Bitcoin. This distinction becomes crucial when market risk appetite diminishes. In an uptrend, crypto treasury stocks typically trade above their net asset value. When market sentiment shifts, these expectations vanish. Many investors begin to prioritize downside protection and balance sheet robustness over growth potential. The premium quickly narrows, often turning into a discount. Therefore, the stock price decline is not only due to the decrease in the value of cryptocurrencies, but also to the shrinking valuation multiples. During periods of heightened risk aversion, funds are more likely to shift from alternative assets such as stocks to ETFs, or even withdraw entirely from the cryptocurrency market, thus accelerating premium compression. This structural shift has resulted in a faster and greater rate of premium compression than in previous cycles.

data.title: A renowned independent crypto analyst released his top ten predictions for 2026.
data.description:
Author: Miles Deutscher & Translation: Jia Huan, ChainCatcher

In this article, I will break down my top 10 cryptocurrency predictions for 2026 in detail. This includes my $BTC price prediction, top altcoin narratives, the prospects of combining crypto with AI, and more. This is an exercise I do every year, and it really helps me calibrate my direction for the new year. Even if you disagree with all the predictions here, I hope it will inspire you to make your own list to prepare for the year ahead and capture the biggest upside potential.

10. Prediction Market Trading Volume to Grow 5x

From January 2025 to today, prediction market trading volume has grown approximately 10x. I believe this trend will continue, and in 2026, we will see at least one month with trading volume reaching 5x current levels.

A fivefold increase in current trading volume would equate to approximately $95 billion per month. Another factor to consider is the rise of “neighbor protocols” built on top of prediction markets. For example, @intodotspace is building the first leveraged prediction market. This trend will further amplify trading volume.

9. Stock/Metal Perpetual Contract Trading Boom

The argument here is simple: we will continue to see perpetual contracts spread across the mainstream—not just limited to crypto token trading.

... 2025 will be a breakout year for perpetual futures technology on crypto tracks—instant settlement, excellent UI/UX, decentralization, and more. Secondly, gold/metals/stocks are in a strong bull market. Typically, as cycles near their peak, people tend to move further down the risk curve in pursuit of returns (perpetual contracts are well-suited to benefit alongside small-cap/emerging markets). In many cases, accessing crypto tracks is easier and faster than buying stocks/gold through traditional financial (TradFi) tracks. Due to this combined effect, I believe we will see an explosive growth in stock/metal trading volume on perpetual DEXs, and perpetual contract trading will extend far beyond crypto token trading volume. 8. The ICO "Renaissance" Continues Due to the dramatic shift in the regulatory environment, the public now has unprecedented opportunities to participate in token sales. ICO participation is at an all-time high, and I expect this overall upward trend to continue. If you're interested in profiting from ICOs, I recently released a complete Tweet Guide and a free Notion template to help you stay organized in this space—you can check it out below.

7. Altcoins with Strong Revenue Models Will Win

The market trend has clearly shifted, favoring genuine businesses over mere speculation and hype. Of course, the latter will always have a place in the market, but more and more investors (large and small) are looking for a real "flywheel" to support token prices (in a sea of ​​token dilution).

I believe protocols that actually generate revenue will continue to dominate. Simply put, protocols with real revenue mechanisms will thrive better than those without. Pay attention to companies/projects/teams that are actually generating revenue.

6. RWA is poised for its second-best year ever. I'd love to say RWA is going to have its biggest year ever. However, technically, it will be difficult to surpass the $14 billion growth projected for 2025. I believe liquidity will continue to flow into the space, making it another bumper year, but the percentage growth will likely be lower than in 2025. 2026 is likely to bring many yield/tokenization opportunities and a significant presence in altcoin trading. 5. Digital Banks (Neobanks) Become the Most Exponentially Growing Sector in Crypto Space It feels like we've finally reached a turning point for crypto/stablecoin banking. The infrastructure has finally caught up with demand, and we're seeing explosive growth in the adoption of many products. The ability to easily exchange cryptocurrencies for fiat currencies is a much-needed solution. This sector has the largest potential market (basically the entire financial world) and is actively addressing issues in developing countries and regions where traditional financial systems are less convenient. This is a huge market, and I expect we will finally (truly) conquer it this year. 4. AI / AI Agents Are Truly Back Last January, we either experienced a crazy AI hype season that drove up the price of Crypto x AI protocols—the only problem was that the technology hadn't caught up yet. Entering 2026, things are different; the technology can actually deliver on the hype's promise. To me, 2026 will undoubtedly be the most pivotal year for AI to date (like every year), and as people speculate on the upside potential of AI, retail interest can easily spill over into the crypto space. Crypto x AI is the perfect synergy. Crypto brings freedom to the financial track, while AI brings automation, which I believe is the future of finance. I think this will create opportunities in many AI sub-sectors, including x402, robotics, agentic workflows, AI data/infrastructure, and more. 3. Stablecoin Supply Increases by Over 50% Last year, the total supply of stablecoins increased by 50% (from $200 billion to $300 billion). I believe we will see a similar increase in stablecoin supply this year, further catalyzed by the Genius Act in the United States, which regulates stablecoins.

2. Institutional Drivers Will Outpace Retail Investors

This entire cycle has been driven by institutions (DATs, ETFs, etc.). I expect this shift towards an institutionally driven market to continue—which is why I'm focusing on tokens/protocols that can attract institutional interest (returning to my point about focusing on projects with real revenue).

1. BTC Price Will Be Higher at Year-End

Whether $BTC will experience a crazy "peak and fall" above $150,000 this year depends on many factors.

Funding flows/buyer demand, DATs, macroeconomic background, etc. Frankly, I'm not sure if the market can align with all the right factors to trigger the most extreme bullish topping pattern. However, I believe Bitcoin will close with a bullish price candle at the end of 2026. This means $BTC must close above the $90,000 range. To summarize my bullish rationale (I may post a more detailed post on this later): We are likely in the final year of a business cycle, and we are seeing similar price bottoming behavior to previous years, which makes me believe we will at least close in the green this year—I will delve deeper into my exact argument in the coming weeks, so stay tuned.
